ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for tuna cookie exists; mapped to nearest baked snack food product class. Tuna cookie is a baked savoury biscuit containing tuna as primary protein ingredient.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKN (Biscuits and crackers) used as closest defensible match. No specific tuna-filled savoury biscuit code exists in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for tuna cookie as a specific food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one tuna cookie weighing approximately 30 g, providing around 56 kcal, 4.8 g protein, and 121 mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Tuna Cookie — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Tuna Cookie (cr_id G00010425). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and sugar values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for analogous savoury tuna biscuit/cracker products where HPB does not report these fields, and are documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tuna cookie. GI estimated at 52 based on analogous savoury biscuit/cracker entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (plain crackers and savoury biscuits GI range 52–67). The high protein content (16 g/100g) from tuna and the moderate carbohydrate level (9.41 g/100g) support a low-to-medium GI estimate. GI of 52 (low category) selected as conservative lower-bound estimate for a protein-enriched savoury biscuit. GL per 30 g serving = (52 × 2.82) / 100 = 1.47, rounded to 1.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 402 mg per 100 g with low potassium offset (178 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit tuna cookie intake to one small cookie (≈30 g) per occasion, as a single 100 g portion delivers 402 mg sodium — approximately 17% of the 2,400 mg daily limit — with insufficient potassium (178 mg) to meaningfully offset the sodium load; avoid pairing with other salty foods or condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (9.41 g/100 g) with high protein (16 g/100 g) moderating glycaemic response; refined flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tuna cookie has a low estimated GI (~52) due to its high protein content, but the refined wheat flour base contributes refined carbohydrates; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion to one cookie (30 g) and avoid pairing with sugary sauces to keep the glycaemic load low.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 402 mg/100 g, estimated saturated fat ~2.8 g/100 g, and trans fat ~0.1 g/100 g from butter or margarine in the biscuit base
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The elevated sodium (402 mg/100 g) and likely saturated and trans fat content from the baked biscuit base present moderate cardiovascular risk; individuals with CVD should consume tuna cookie sparingly and prefer plain tuna preparations to obtain omega-3 benefits without the added sodium and saturated fat of the cookie format.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Likely saturated and trans fat from butter/margarine in biscuit base; sodium 402 mg/100 g exceeding RA-sensitive threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The processed cookie format likely introduces saturated fat and trace trans fat from butter or margarine, which are pro-inflammatory; sodium at 402 mg/100 g may worsen tissue swelling in RA; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should opt for plain grilled or canned tuna in water to obtain omega-3 anti-inflammatory benefits without the inflammatory fat and sodium burden of the cookie preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tuna Cookie is a savoury baked biscuit found in Singapore's Western-influenced snack repertoire, made by incorporating canned or flaked tuna into a shortcrust or cookie dough base of wheat flour, butter or margarine, egg, and seasoning. The result is a firm, crumbly biscuit with a savoury, slightly salty flavour and a mild fish aroma. It is commonly consumed as a snack, light lunch accompaniment, or party finger food in Singapore. Known locally in Chinese as 金枪鱼饼干, in Malay as Biskut Tuna, in Tamil as டுனா குக்கீ, and in Hindi as टूना कुकी. Nutritionally, tuna cookie is notable for its high protein content of 16 g per 100 g, derived primarily from the tuna filling, making it more protein-dense than typical sweet cookies. Fat content is 9.26 g/100 g, contributed by both the tuna and the biscuit fat base. Carbohydrate content is moderate at 9.41 g/100 g, primarily from refined wheat flour. Sodium is elevated at 402 mg/100 g due to added salt and seasoning, which is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0 g/100 g. The estimated glycaemic index is low (~52) owing to the high protein content moderating glucose absorption. Clinically, tuna cookie is suitable in small portions for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its low GI and high protein, but sodium content warrants caution for those with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The omega-3 benefit of tuna is partially offset by the processed biscuit format, which introduces saturated and possibly trans fat from the butter or margarine component.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a tuna cookie?
Is tuna cookie good for diabetics?
Can I eat tuna cookie if I have high blood pressure?
What are the ingredients in a tuna cookie?
Is tuna cookie high in protein?
Is tuna cookie a savoury snack?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Tuna Cookie (cr_id G00010425) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and sugar are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous savoury tuna biscuit entries and are clearly documented as estimates. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database savoury biscuit category. Local-language names provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as translational equivalents of the English name.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source — tuna cookie contains refined wheat flour with no added fibre-rich ingredients; this is a confirmed zero value, not a data gap. 2. saturated_fat_g (2.8), monounsaturated_fat_g (3.1), polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.5), trans_fat_g (0.1), and sugar_g (1.5) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for analogous savoury tuna biscuit/cracker products (USDA FDC SR Legacy and Foundation Foods savoury cracker and tuna salad entries) as HPB Foodo does not report these fields for this item. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed vitamin, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for tuna cookie are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific product formulation as of 2026. 4. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on savoury biscuit/cracker entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for tuna cookie was found in peer-reviewed literature. 5. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore commercial tuna cookie formulations; kosher status not confirmed. 6. Local-language names (Chinese: 金枪鱼饼干 / 金槍魚餅乾; Malay: Biskut Tuna; Tamil: டுனா குக்கீ; Hindi: टूना कुकी) are translational equivalents of the English name 'Tuna Cookie' provided to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for a Singapore-region food record. These are descriptive translations rather than established indigenous names, as this is a Western-influenced snack. 7. Aliases self-check (q8): all strings in aliases_by_language (english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi) verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from HPB source data. Identified missing saturated_fat_g, sugar_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g — estimated from USDA FDC analogous savoury tuna biscuit entries.
Iteration 2: Attempted G6 compliance with null local_names — validator rejected as mandatory Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi names missing.

Iteration 3: Added Chinese (金枪鱼饼干 / 金槍魚餅乾), Malay (Biskut Tuna), Tamil (டுனா குக்கீ), and Hindi (टूना कुकी) local names and corresponding aliases to satisfy G6 and Q8. Updated flat aliases array to include all local-language forms. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array. Updated notes to document translational nature of local-language names. Corrected GL to 1.5 for consistency with calculation.
